verdict.answer_first: A: sentence 1 says the core immediately, "Hoisting is JavaScript's behavior of moving declarations"; B first has a title before the same definition.
verdict.decisiveness: A: it compresses to one governing rule, "declaration is hoisted, but the assignment stays put"; B adds a survey table, which is useful but less singular.
verdict.actionability: B: the "Quick reference" table makes the behavior/error per declaration fastest to apply.
